# AI Coding Assistants Executive Summary

Use this as the one-screen briefing for why the talk exists. The market has moved beyond autocomplete towards repo-aware, tool-using assistants, but the public-sector test is whether the workflow can be governed.

## Source Slice

- Source path: `research/hmrc-beyond-hype/import/AI coding assistants on 9 May 2026 for the HMRC Data Science Academy talk.md`.
- Source section: [Executive summary](../../import/AI%20coding%20assistants%20on%209%20May%202026%20for%20the%20HMRC%20Data%20Science%20Academy%20talk.md#executive-summary).
- Source line range in the current import file: `5-16`.
- Source status: point-in-time public-source snapshot dated 9 May 2026; recheck fast-moving product claims before live use.

## Talk Use

- Open the market context after the dark-data problem: the question is not only which model is strongest, but which assisted workflow a team can control.
- Frame the Challenge 2 repo as evidence of a source-backed, human-governed workflow rather than a claim that AI can replace engineering ownership.
- Keep the safe starting point explicit: supervised assistance on lower-risk, synthetic, or sanitised material first.
